title: audio<->retrieval cross-group swap latency (llama-swap exclusive groups)

gpu.note: peak occurs while the 8GB embed/rerank member is resident plus swap transients; no OOM at any point

verdict.retrieval_to_audio: cold TTS 6.8s warm-cache / 9.8s cold-cache end-to-end; swap+load component ~4.6s (warm-cache) — within the ~5-8s plan expectation warm-cache, ~1.8s over it on a fully cold page cache
verdict.audio_to_retrieval: first search after TTS costs only ~0.18s more than steady state (4.69s vs 4.51s) — the D13 per-search intra-group swap dominates; the direct embed component is 2.65s warm-cache / 4.59s cold-cache, well under the ~6-10s expectation
verdict.stt_session_start_d10: ws connect -> stt_status listening: 4.43s median cold member / 0.58s warm / 5.10s worst cold-page-cache — 6x headroom under the 30s D10 ceiling, inside the ~5-8s plan expectation
verdict.vram: peak 14336 MiB during swaps (budget 16380) — never overlapped groups into an OOM

methodology.real_requests: All primary numbers are production requests through the Orchestrator (:9091): POST /tts/batch voice=qwen:Vivian (short 1-chunk text, constant across cold/warm so cold-minus-warm isolates swap+load), GET /debug/context?query=... (the production hybrid retrieval pipeline: query embed via active provider qwen3-embedding-8b-local + localstack rerank-qwen3-8b over candidates), and a real /ws/stt onbox session timed from ws connect to stt_status{listening} (the D10 warm+prime path).
methodology.state_setting: Residency forced between reps via :9098 (direct embed to land the retrieval group / TTS to land the audio group) and verified with llama-swap /running before and after every measured request.
methodology.reps: 3 reps per direction, median + range reported. Warm baselines measured immediately after each cold rep (ttl=600 so no idle-unload interference).
methodology.page_cache: Primary numbers ran with model files in the OS page cache (steady-state box). A second pass dropped caches (sync; echo 3 > drop_caches) before EACH single measurement for true cold-disk numbers.
methodology.component_corroboration: diagnostics/localstack/swap_cost.py (committed G5 harness) run from the Orchestrator venv: --scope cross and --scope intra, --cache warm, 5 iters.
methodology.driver: scratch driver /tmp/g5_driver.py + /tmp/g5_cold_rep.py + /tmp/g5_stt_once.py on MS02 (measurement-only; no repo changes)

1_retrieval_resident_to_first_tts.note: cold wall = evict retrieval member + boot qwen-tts uvicorn + load 1.7B variant + synth; plan expectation ~5-8s: met warm-cache (6.8s), exceeded by ~1.8s only on a fully dropped page cache (9.8s)

2_audio_resident_to_first_search.endpoint: GET :9091/debug/context (production hybrid retrieve: keyword + semantic embed + rerank)
2_audio_resident_to_first_search.note: warm is BARELY cheaper than cold: every search pays the D13 sequential-retrieval intra-group swap (embed loads evicting rerank, then rerank loads evicting embed), so evicting the audio group first adds only ~0.18s. Steady-state search cost on this box is ~4.5s wall.

3_stt_session_start_d10.endpoint: WS :9091/ws/stt stt_start -> stt_status{loading_models} -> stt_status{listening} (default onbox VAD loop; _warm_and_prime warms the audio group via /upstream/speaches/health then primes whisper with 0.2s silence)
3_stt_session_start_d10.loading_affordance: stt_status{loading_models} arrived 14-44 ms after connect in every session (cold and warm)
3_stt_session_start_d10.note: cold = speaches process down (retrieval group resident); llama-swap boots speaches (static :9099) + whisper load + prime. Worst case incl. dropped page cache 5.10s -> ~6x headroom under the 30s D10 ceiling; warm sessions re-pay only the prime (~0.58s).
